task: Inspect returned equipment at check-in, document damage or missing components against the outgoing condition record, and open repair follow-up before the unit enters the service queue.
frequency: every return cycle through the branch
tool_today: Check-in inspection workflow, condition reports, mobile photos, work-order screen
decision_content: Whether the condition is normal wear, immediate repair work, or a customer-damage issue that needs escalation before the unit can move forward.

task: Execute preventive-maintenance work from the PM schedule, complete the required service steps, and close the PM work order with the actual maintenance performed.
frequency: throughout the day as units come due
tool_today: PM schedule, OEM service checklist, work-order system
decision_content: Whether a unit can safely wait for its PM slot or must be pulled now to avoid a larger failure or a missed compliance interval.

task: Diagnose and repair mechanical, hydraulic, and electrical faults on rental equipment and escalate work that exceeds branch skill, time, or authorization limits.
frequency: multiple repairs per shift in busy periods
tool_today: Work-order queue, diagnostic tools, OEM manuals, shop test equipment
decision_content: Whether the observed failure is the root cause, whether the branch can repair it in-house, and when the scope or cost needs escalation.

task: Record labor, parts used, repair notes, and work-order status updates so the service queue and equipment history stay current.
frequency: after each work order or major repair step
tool_today: Technician mobile app or shop terminal, work-order screen, parts lookup
decision_content: Whether the record is complete enough to support the next technician, the manager, and the future service history without a later reconstruction pass.

task: Prepare repaired equipment for return to service by cleaning it, confirming decals and accessories, and updating it to a rent-ready status only when the open deficiencies are resolved.
frequency: for each completed repair or service cycle
tool_today: Final inspection checklist, wash bay, equipment status screen
decision_content: Whether the unit is genuinely ready to rent again or still needs follow-up work that should keep it unavailable.

task: Manage parts for the active repair queue by checking stock, sourcing required components, and handling warranty-processing follow-up tied to completed service work.
tool_today: Parts inventory, vendor catalogs, purchase-order flow, warranty paperwork
decision_content: Whether to wait for routine replenishment, pay for an expedited part, or escalate a blocked job that is threatening availability.

task: Respond to field service calls at customer jobsites, diagnose the failure on site, and document whether the issue was repaired in the field or needs a swap or return-to-shop follow-up.
frequency: when on-rent equipment fails or urgent service is requested
tool_today: Service van, field-service ticket, phone with dispatch, mobile work-order update
decision_content: Whether the repair is safe and economical to complete on site or whether the customer needs a swap, pickup, or deeper shop repair instead.

task: Complete scheduled safety and compliance inspections, record pass-fail results on standardized checklists, and keep the asset record audit-ready for internal and external review.
frequency: scheduled inspection windows plus return-to-service events
tool_today: Inspection checklist, compliance calendar, maintenance record system
decision_content: Whether the equipment passes as-is, needs corrective work before release, or should be held out of service until a compliance gap is resolved.
